title: Commentary on Investments: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total investments declined to $3,090 as of March 31, 2026, reflecting a decrease from the previous period. The portfolio remains structured as shown and continues to be heavily weighted toward time deposits, which total $2,490 and represent approximately 81% of the investment portfolio.

Other investments totaled $600 and remain immaterial relative to the overall portfolio. Time deposits decreased by -7.5% from the previous quarter's balance, a reduction that reflects active management decisions regarding liquidity needs or interest rate positioning within the broader asset-liability framework.
The investment maturity structure remains well distributed across multiple time horizons, supporting effective liquidity management and interest rate risk control. The mix of maturities allows for flexibility in meeting short-term obligations while capturing yield opportunities over varying durations.
Overall, Northern Valley's investment portfolio remains conservative, liquid, and aligned with policy guidelines. Management continues to emphasize capital preservation, stable yield generation, and prudent duration management while maintaining flexibility to respond to changing balance-sheet needs and market opportunities.
